tool.name: dashing2 wsketch
tool.description: sketch weighted sets of integral identifiers
tool.command: ## wsketch writes several files named after the -o prefix, so we sketch ## into a dedicated directory and discover the results as a collection. mkdir -p wsout && dashing2 wsketch -p \${GALAXY_SLOTS:-8} -S${sketch_size} $sketch_type #if $weights.mode == 'ids': $id_size #end if #if $weights.mode != 'ids': $weights.weight_type #end if #if $weights.mode == 'csr': $weights.indptr_size #end if -o 'wsout/sketch' #if $weights.mode == 'ids': '$ids' #elif $weights.mode == 'weighted': '$ids' '$weights.weights_file' #else: '$weights.data_file' '$ids' '$weights.indptr_file' #end if

param.name: ids
param.label: Identifier / index stream
param.help: A raw binary stream of integral identifiers (indices). 64-bit by default; enable '32-bit identifiers' below if they are 32-bit. In CSR mode this is the indices array.

param.name: mode
param.label: Weighting
param.help: How weights are supplied. With identifiers only, every element is sketched with equal weight (SetSketch). With a weight vector, a 1-D weighted MinHash is performed. CSR mode treats a (data, indices, indptr) triple as a sparse matrix and emits one sketch per row.

param.name: weights_file
param.label: Weight vector
param.help: A raw binary vector of weights, one per identifier. 64-bit double by default; choose a different type below.

param.name: weight_type
param.label: Weight data type
param.help: Element type of the weight vector.

param.name: data_file
param.label: CSR data array
param.help: The non-zero values of the sparse matrix (the weights), in CSR order.

param.name: indptr_file
param.label: CSR indptr array
param.help: The CSR row-pointer array. 64-bit integers by default.

param.name: weight_type
param.label: Data (weight) type
param.help: Element type of the CSR data array.

param.name: indptr_size
param.label: 32-bit indptr
param.help: Read the indptr array as 32-bit integers instead of 64-bit.

param.name: id_size
param.label: 32-bit identifiers
param.help: Read the identifier stream as 32-bit integers instead of 64-bit. (Identifiers-only mode.)

param.name: sketch_size
param.label: Sketch size
param.help: Number of registers retained per sketch.

param.name: sketch_type
param.label: Sketching algorithm
param.help: ProbMinHash treats the input as a discrete probability distribution (default). BagMinHash treats it as a multiset. SetSketch ignores weights and treats it as a set.

tool.help:
dashing2 wsketch hashes **weighted sets of integral identifiers** directly, without any sequence parsing. It is a specialist tool: its inputs are raw binary streams of integers (and, optionally, weights), not FASTA/FASTQ. For weighted sketching of *sequence* data, use the *dashing2 sketch* tool with the ``--multiset`` or ``--prob`` mode instead.
-----
Input *****
One to three raw binary arrays, depending on the weighting mode:
=========================== ===================================================== **Mode** **Arrays (in order)** --------------------------- ----------------------------------------------------- Identifiers only identifiers (equal weight, SetSketch) Identifiers + weights identifiers, weight vector Sparse matrix (CSR) data, indices, indptr (one sketch per row) =========================== =====================================================
Identifiers and indptr are 64-bit integers by default; weights are 64-bit doubles by default. Each can be switched to a smaller type with the options provided.
-----
Options *******
=========================== ========== =========== =========================== **Option** **Flag** **Default** **Description** --------------------------- ---------- ----------- --------------------------- Sketch size -S 1024 Registers per sketch Sketching algorithm -B / -q ProbMinHash Weight handling 32-bit identifiers -u False Identifier element size Weight data type -f/-U/-H double Weight element size 32-bit indptr -P False CSR indptr element size =========================== ========== =========== ===========================
-----
Output ******
A collection of the binary files written by wsketch (sampled indices, hashes, identifiers and a total-weight summary), named by the suffixes dashing2 assigns. There is no native Galaxy datatype for these, so they are stored as ``binary``.
